findings.description: Design.md 'Phase-by-phase dispatch matrix' (lines 411-425) and spec scenario 'All 13 non-terminal phases dispatch with resolved archetype' (specs/skill-workflow/spec.md line 41) promise that PLAN_FIX/IMPL_FIX/VAL_FIX phases record phase_archetype 'via the convergence loop's existing audit path'. That audit path does not exist: `grep phase_archetype skills/autopilot/scripts/convergence_loop.py` returns zero matches. No task in tasks.md adds this wiring. As written, every *_FIX phase will leave LoopState.phase_archetype as None on real runs, violating the spec scenario at line 45 ('values for ... PLAN_FIX, IMPL_FIX, ... VAL_FIX SHALL each match the configured phase_mapping archetype').

phase_archetype length constraint mismatch. The contracts SQL migration adds `CHECK (LENGTH(phase_archetype) <= 32)` (contracts/db/0NN_add_phase_archetype.sql:21), but the existing API model already has `Field(default=None, max_length=64)` at agent-coordinator/src/coordination_api.py:221. A 33-64-char value would pass API validation and fail the DB CHECK, raising IntegrityError at heartbeat time. Either tighten the API to max_length=32 (recommended; matches longest archetype name 'implementer'=11 chars with comfortable headroom) or relax the DB to <=64.

Token-budget threshold inconsistency across artifacts. proposal.md:230-231 says 'fails the build if any phase's joined prompt exceeds 75% of the resolved model's context window, and warns at 60%'. design.md:375 risks table says 'CI check at 80% of model context window'. tasks.md:117-119 says 'fails (exit 1) at >75%, warns at 60-75%, passes silently below 60%'. Pick one set of thresholds and update the other two artifacts.

_resolve_phase_archetype_for_state_only resolution path is underspecified. design.md D7 (line 286-298) introduces the function but does not state whether it (a) calls coordination_bridge.try_resolve_archetype_for_phase (which can return None on coordinator failure), or (b) reads phase_mapping locally from a static source. The spec scenario at specs/skill-workflow/spec.md:56-61 requires INIT to record 'runner' deterministically. If implementation chooses (a), spec scenario fails when coordinator is unreachable; if (b), there is no source-of-truth file specified. Recommend explicit decision: state-only resolver SHALL load archetype name from agent-coordinator/archetypes.yaml::phase_mapping (or a vendored copy) without HTTP calls, since INIT/SUBMIT_PR have empty signal lists and need no coordinator-side dynamic resolution.

Goal G1 in design.md (line 28-29) overclaims: 'Every non-terminal autopilot phase actually dispatches with the archetype-resolved model and system_prompt.' The dispatch matrix at lines 411-425 shows only 7 of 13 non-terminal phases dispatch a sub-agent (PLAN_ITERATE, PLAN_REVIEW, IMPLEMENT, IMPL_ITERATE, IMPL_REVIEW, VALIDATE, VAL_REVIEW). INIT, PLAN, PLAN_FIX, IMPL_FIX, VAL_FIX, SUBMIT_PR do not. Reword G1 as 'Every sub-agent-dispatching phase' or 'Every phase that resolves an archetype records it on LoopState'.

Task 2.9b assumes a CI gate that imports _build_prompt for every dispatching phase to compute token counts, but offers no recipe for constructing the synthetic state_dict and incoming PhaseRecord required by the prompt assembler. _build_prompt at skills/autopilot/scripts/phase_agent.py:303 requires 'incoming_handoff: PhaseRecord' and 'artifacts_manifest: list[str] | None'. Without a deterministic fixture, the CI gate's measurements are not reproducible. Either specify a fixture file path or make the gate a runtime sampler instead of a static check.

Task 2.9b creates a new `skills/autopilot/scripts/token_budget_check.py` while skills/autopilot/scripts/phase_token_meter.py already exposes `measure_context()`. Duplicating context-measurement logic risks drift between the runtime meter and the CI gate. Reuse phase_token_meter (or extend it) rather than introducing a parallel measurement path.

Backward-compat claim mismatch. proposal.md Impact section (line 219-221) says 'No user-facing API breakage: All API additions are optional fields; older clients continue to function.' But task 3.9 tightens StatusReportRequest from `phase_archetype: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=64)` to `Literal["architect","reviewer","implementer","analyst","runner"] | None = None`. Any prior client that sent an arbitrary string (including older agentic-coding-tools versions experimenting with custom archetype names) will now get HTTP 422 instead of 200. Either soften task 3.9 to accept-but-warn, or update the proposal Impact statement to call out the validation tightening as a (likely safe but real) breaking change.

proposal.md 'What Changes' item 4 (line 64-69) describes the INIT-phase archetype recorder as `_resolve_phase_archetype_for_state_only` but only mentions INIT. design.md D7 (line 286) and the dispatch matrix list both INIT and SUBMIT_PR as state-only phases needing the resolver. Update item 4 to mention both phases explicitly.

Task 3.8 wording is misleading. It says 'extend the StatusReportRequest Pydantic model to include phase_archetype: str | None = None'. That field already exists at agent-coordinator/src/coordination_api.py:221 (added by the archived per-phase-archetype-resolution change). The actual gap is in the request handler at coordination_api.py:1980-2010: `discovery.heartbeat(agent_id=request.agent_id)` is called WITHOUT phase_archetype, and the field is only forwarded into the event-bus context (not persisted to the agent_sessions row). Reword task 3.8 to say: 'Verify that StatusReportRequest is already extended; if not, extend it. Then wire request.phase_archetype through DiscoveryService.heartbeat(phase_archetype=...) so it persists in the agent_sessions row, not just emitted via the event bus.'

Task 4.1 asserts that '.phase-resolution-cache.json is created and deleted between consecutive phases (assert it does not exist after apply_phase_outcome succeeds)'. INIT and SUBMIT_PR are state-only and do not invoke build_phase_dispatch_kwargs (per dispatch matrix), so no cache file is ever created for those phases. The assertion needs a per-phase carve-out or the test will fail on the state-only phases. Reword: 'For each of the 7 sub-agent-dispatching phases, the cache file SHALL be created during build-dispatch and SHALL not exist after apply-outcome.'

design.md D2 (line 121-123) says 'Test 2.9 covers this case explicitly with a phase prompt containing `---` and asserts (a) the separator after the system prompt is exactly the SEPARATOR constant string and (b) key task-instruction keywords from `phase_prompt` survive the fold unchanged. This is asserted via regex (not just substring presence).' But the actual test in tasks.md 2.9 (line 96-101) says 'assert via regex that (a) exactly one occurrence of the literal SEPARATOR `"

---

"` appears'. If the phase prompt itself contains the literal separator, 'exactly one occurrence' is wrong: there will be at least two. Reword to: 'the joined prompt begins with system_prompt + SEPARATOR (anchored to start), and the substring after that anchor matches the original phase_prompt verbatim'.

Cache schema includes `schema_version: 1` (design.md line 218) but no validation behavior is defined for version mismatch. The validation list at lines 235-244 covers parse error, change_id mismatch, phase mismatch, and checksum mismatch — but a future cache file with `schema_version: 2` would currently pass all those checks if the field set still includes change_id+phase+archetype+checksum. Add: 'If schema_version != 1, treat as parse error: write phase_archetype=None and warn.'

VAL_REVIEW is conditionally enabled by the existing `val_review_enabled` flag in LoopState (also shown in schemas/SKILL.md). Task 2.10 lists VAL_REVIEW as one of the 7 phases that gets a SKILL.md dispatch block but does not specify how the dispatch block handles the `val_review_enabled = false` case. The block needs an explicit guard: 'if val_review_enabled is false, skip dispatch and transition straight to next phase'. Otherwise the rewrite breaks `--no-review` autopilot runs.

Migration sequence-number coordination is hand-wavy. contracts/db/0NN_add_phase_archetype.sql comment (lines 4-7) says the implementer is responsible for choosing max+1 'and verifying no parallel PR has claimed the same number'. No automated check is specified. Last migration on main is 022_add_missing_profile_operations.sql so this PR will land 023. If a parallel PR also lands 023, both migrations clash silently in the migration runner. Add a CI gate that fails when two migration files share a numeric prefix, or pin the rename to wp-coordinator-status-discovery's verification step.

Spec text (specs/skill-workflow/spec.md line 16) lists '13 non-terminal phases': INIT, PLAN, PLAN_ITERATE, PLAN_REVIEW, PLAN_FIX, IMPLEMENT, IMPL_ITERATE, IMPL_REVIEW, IMPL_FIX, VALIDATE, VAL_REVIEW, VAL_FIX, SUBMIT_PR. This matches the events schema (contracts/events/status-report.schema.json) which adds ESCALATE, DONE as terminals. Good. But the modified requirement at spec line 18 then says 'SHALL invoke the harness Agent(...) tool for the following 7 phases' — explicit and clear. The contradiction window is closed by the dispatch matrix in design.md. Recommend cross-linking the spec line 18 list to design.md's dispatch matrix to make the 7-vs-13 distinction immediately discoverable inside the spec itself.

Migration uses CONSTRAINT phase_archetype_enum CHECK with the literal list ('architect','reviewer','implementer','analyst','runner'). Adding/removing an archetype in agent-coordinator/archetypes.yaml will silently diverge from this DB constraint until the next migration. No test asserts that archetypes.yaml's set of archetypes equals the DB CHECK list. Add a small test or CI step (in wp-contracts or wp-coordinator-status-discovery's verification) that loads archetypes.yaml and asserts the archetype set equals the CHECK list parsed from the migration file, so future archetype additions trigger a CI failure pointing to the SQL.

Risk 'Multi-agent safety: cache is per-change' (design.md line 250-253) acknowledges that two concurrent autopilot runs against the same change would clobber the cache, but says 'highly unusual'. Convergence-loop reviews (parallel-review-plan, parallel-review-implementation) dispatch multiple vendor agents in parallel for the SAME phase under the same change-id — this is not unusual, it is the normal case. The cache file as designed is single-writer. Either (a) make the cache key per-(change_id, phase) so concurrent phases don't collide, or (b) confirm explicitly that build_phase_dispatch_kwargs is only called from autopilot's sequential prose driver and never from the parallel review path, and document why.

tasks.md 4.3 covers three failure modes for inline fallback (Agent unavailable, coordinator 503, TimeoutError) but does not cover the 'apply_phase_outcome called when cache file is missing entirely' case. design.md line 235 says 'Parse the JSON. On parse error → write phase_archetype=None'. A missing file should be treated similarly. Add an explicit test scenario for cache-file-not-found.

Proposal/design refer to the migration target as 'agent-status table' (proposal.md line 24, design.md line 304), but the actual table name is `agent_sessions` (verified at agent-coordinator/database/migrations/001_core_schema.sql:79 and 003_agent_discovery.sql which adds discovery columns). The contracts SQL file uses agent_sessions correctly. Update proposal/design prose to use 'agent_sessions table' so reviewers don't grep for a non-existent agent-status table.
